title: The Greatest Story Ever Dohed

jokes.text: Now let us download the holy tweet of the Lord.
jokes.setup: Modern attempt to make Bible study relevant with technology references
jokes.punchline: Combining sacred language with social media terminology
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Lovejoy trying to be hip but sounding awkward. Solid wordplay mixing religious and tech language.

jokes.text: Stop making this relatable. As a youth, if I don't hear a computer word every sentence, I am outta here.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy trying to appeal to youth with tech references
jokes.punchline: Bart mockingly demanding even more pandering
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary on how institutions pander to youth. Perfect Bart character moment - sarcastic and manipulative.

jokes.text: Mousepad! Double click! Skype! Skype!
jokes.setup: Bart's sarcastic demand for more tech words
jokes.punchline: Lovejoy desperately shouting random tech terms
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and desperation comedy. The repeated 'Skype!' is particularly funny - shows Lovejoy's panic.

jokes.text: My bathing suit came off!
jokes.punchline: Random pool accident disruption
jokes.explanation: Simple slapstick interruption. Gets a reaction but not particularly clever or character-driven.

jokes.text: Schnell! Schnell!
jokes.punchline: German stereotypical efficiency/urgency
jokes.explanation: Basic national stereotype humor. Functional but relies on familiar trope.

jokes.text: You are the most infuriating, Netflix DVD-burglaring, barbecue-not-putting-out... man I ever met!
jokes.setup: Marge building up anger at Homer
jokes.punchline: Mixing serious complaints with oddly specific grievances
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Marge's specific complaints are both relatable and absurd. 'Netflix DVD-burglaring' is particularly creative.

jokes.text: I sense a 'but' coming. Here comes the butt!
jokes.setup: Lovejoy about to make encouraging point about redemption
jokes.punchline: Homer's childish pun on 'but/butt'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - interrupting serious conversation with juvenile humor. Predictable but in character.

jokes.text: What about Sodom and Gomorrah? He lovingly destroyed them.
jokes.setup: Discussion about God never giving up on anyone
jokes.punchline: Lovejoy's euphemistic description of divine destruction
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy. 'Lovingly destroyed' is a perfect oxymoron that captures religious rationalization humor.

jokes.text: Let me think... Take my family to a war zone, on a bus filled with religious lame-os, in a country with no pork, in a desert with no casinos... Where do I sign up?
jokes.setup: Ned inviting family to Holy Land
jokes.punchline: Homer listing everything wrong then expressing enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - cataloging all the reasons to hate something then agreeing anyway. Great character consistency and structure.

jokes.text: I can hear your sarcasm from inside the house. And the dishwasher's on!
jokes.punchline: Marge emphasizing how loud his sarcasm is despite noise
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about how obvious sarcasm can be. The dishwasher detail adds specificity.

jokes.text: Great. More hell for me.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's pessimistic worldview
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa character moment - her dry pessimism delivered efficiently. Very much in character.

jokes.text: I also want to try something called 'pita bread.'
jokes.punchline: Admitting mundane curiosity about ethnic food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned moment - his sheltered Midwestern curiosity about common food. Shows his innocence.

jokes.text: The man hates pants.
jokes.setup: Marge telling Homer to put on pants
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact observation about Homer's quirks
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons domestic humor. Marge's resigned acceptance of Homer's weirdness delivered perfectly deadpan.

jokes.text: Latkes aren't as good as American pancakes. They're okay with apple sauce. True enough. A truckload of apple sauce!
jokes.punchline: Escalating to absurd quantity of condiment needed
jokes.explanation: Good Homer food humor - starts diplomatic then reveals his true gluttonous thoughts with the escalation.

jokes.text: Don't want to end up in hell. Jews don't believe in hell. No hell? Thank you, kid. You made my day.
jokes.punchline: Immediate relief upon learning his religion has no hell
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment for Krusty - his relief is palpable and funny. Good religious humor that's informative too.

jokes.text: You may notice I speak the English with a slight accent. This is because, as you've probably guessed, I used to live in London, and that is why I sound like Hugh Grant.
jokes.punchline: Absurd explanation for clearly Middle Eastern accent
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection joke. Sets up expectation of explaining obvious accent, then gives completely wrong explanation with confidence.

jokes.text: We finish early, we go to jewelry store owned by my cousins. No joke. Is true. Great prices. You got to haggle. Otherwise they don't respect you!
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivoting to family business promotion
jokes.explanation: Good character work - mixing professional obligations with family loyalty. The haggling advice adds authenticity.

jokes.text: What are you talking about? It's the safest country in the world. The only danger here is dehydration. Drink water. Don't die. Looks bad on me.
jokes.setup: Tourist asking about safety in obviously dangerous region
jokes.punchline: Guide's absurd reassurance ending with selfish concern
jokes.explanation: Great dark comedy about dangerous tourist destinations. The selfish ending ('looks bad on me') is perfect.

jokes.text: Who wrote your brochure? The same schmuck who wrote the New Testament?
jokes.punchline: Guide comparing tourism marketing to religious text
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversive humor. Equating tourism brochures with sacred texts is both blasphemous and insightful about marketing.

jokes.text: Shalom is the aloha of this place.
jokes.punchline: Reducing ancient greeting to tourist comparison
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about tourist experiences. Simple but effective parallel between greetings.

jokes.text: Everybody died there for nothing. Okay, first stop: Old City.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they won't visit Masada
jokes.punchline: Casually dismissing historical tragedy
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective humor about historical sites. Guide's matter-of-fact dismissal of tragedy is both shocking and funny.

jokes.text: Where BC turned into AD! Let's go!
jokes.punchline: Oversimplifying complex religious history to calendar change
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist reduction of religious significance to bureaucratic calendar change. Efficiently delivered.

jokes.text: After we see the stations of the omelet bar.
jokes.setup: Ned suggesting stations of the cross
jokes.punchline: Homer's food-focused parallel
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - turning religious pilgrimage into food quest. Good wordplay structure.

jokes.text: These blueberry muffins are bigger than the ones we had at Pechanga. Finally, something you like better than Pechanga.
jokes.setup: Homer comparing food to previous vacation
jokes.punchline: Marge noting this is his first positive comparison
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Homer's obsession with comparing everything to previous experiences, especially food.

jokes.text: Also, where your Jesus, who was a native-born Israeli, sandals, circumcised, perfect English accent, the whole deal.
jokes.setup: Tour guide describing Jesus at the Last Supper location
jokes.punchline: Mixing accurate historical details with absurd anachronisms
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor mixing historical accuracy with obvious anachronisms. 'Perfect English accent' is particularly funny.

jokes.text: You're not part of the group. No listening! It's Israel. It's free country. Okay, that's it. Because of him, no Armenian quarter. Forget it. It's gone.
jokes.setup: Random person listening to free tour
jokes.punchline: Guide's extreme punishment affecting whole group
jokes.explanation: Good escalation comedy - minor infraction leading to disproportionate group punishment. Captures arbitrary authority humor.

jokes.text: Okay, that's it. Because of him, no Armenian quarter. Forget it. It's gone. All thanks to Mr. Listen-for-free.
jokes.setup: Tour guide frustrated with freeloading listener
jokes.punchline: Punishing entire group by canceling tour stops
jokes.explanation: Good tour guide behavior - petty punishment escalation

jokes.text: He's going for the record!
jokes.setup: Homer presumably doing something inappropriate at holy site
jokes.punchline: Someone treating Homer's behavior like a competitive sport
jokes.explanation: Implies visual gag of Homer being inappropriate. Treating bad behavior like sports record adds absurd perspective.

jokes.text: I'm just here to see some sights, try goofy new food and spread some shekels with my Carolina Panthers credit card. Panthers!
jokes.punchline: Reducing sacred pilgrimage to typical American tourism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character work - completely missing spiritual significance, focusing on consumption and random sports team loyalty.

jokes.text: Panthers!
jokes.setup: Homer mentioning Carolina Panthers credit card
jokes.punchline: Random fan enthusiasm in holy land
jokes.explanation: Simple recognition humor, shows American culture spreading everywhere

jokes.text: If you say it like Dracula, I will. Please... That is going right on stupidflanders.org.
jokes.setup: Ned asking Homer to turn off camera
jokes.punchline: Homer's oddly specific condition and threat to post online
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor - random Dracula condition and the idea of a website dedicated to mocking Flanders.

jokes.text: That is going right on stupidflanders.org.
jokes.setup: Ned doing something embarrassing on camera
jokes.punchline: Homer's petty website for mocking Ned
jokes.explanation: Perfect modern Homer petty behavior - social media humiliation tactics

jokes.text: Boy, are you lousy at reading minds.
jokes.setup: Ned claiming to understand what Homer really needs
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt assessment of Ned's empathy
jokes.explanation: Good Homer bluntness moment. Efficiently delivers his obliviousness to Ned's good intentions.

jokes.text: I'm not. It's my ring tone. Yello. This is he. I wanted to know, do you deliver falafels to the top of Mount Zion? Great. I'd like a large falafel with pepperoni, sausage, and extra cheese. Yes, I know what a falafel is.
jokes.setup: Homer's phone ringing during spiritual moment
jokes.punchline: Ordering completely wrong ingredients for Middle Eastern food
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer moment - cultural ignorance combined with confidence. Ordering pizza toppings on falafel while insisting he knows what it is.

jokes.text: Dear Lord: Please, fix the following parking tickets: a647253, sp9, 0325, Shelbyville 932871...
jokes.setup: Homer writing prayer at Wailing Wall
jokes.punchline: Using sacred space for mundane bureaucratic requests
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - completely missing the spiritual significance, treating God like customer service. The specific ticket numbers add realism.

jokes.text: Sad. Never gonna happen. Maybe if you were Brad Pitt.
jokes.punchline: Cynical assessment of divine intervention based on celebrity status
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about divine favoritism and celebrity culture. Efficiently delivered cynicism.

jokes.text: Reading prayers and ignoring them, just like God.
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to indifferent deity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy. Perfect Bart moment - cynical beyond his years. Excellent theological humor about divine indifference.

jokes.text: Dear Lord, don't let Marge find out the hotel leaves chocolates on the pillows at night.
jokes.punchline: Revealing his secret hotel chocolate consumption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - using prayer for trivial guilty conscience. The specificity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Give my dad another heart attack?
jokes.punchline: Someone wishing harm on their father
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about family dysfunction. Works as shock value but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Smite him like the firstborn of Egypt. Dead baby, dead baby, passover, dead baby, dead baby...
jokes.punchline: Escalating to biblical genocide references
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation from Lisa. Shows her knowledge of scripture used for violent purposes. Controversial but in character.

jokes.text: Why am I running from a girl? Call me when you're old enough for your bat mitzvah. I'll bring you an envelope full of nothin'.
jokes.setup: Bart being chased by local girl
jokes.punchline: Questioning his masculinity then making cultural threat
jokes.explanation: Good Bart moment - wounded pride leading to petty threat. Shows cultural awareness used spitefully.

jokes.text: Karate! Krav maga! Krav ma-wha? Israeli karate. Less 'hyah', More 'hyah'!
jokes.punchline: Girl correcting him with more effective martial art
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup. Cultural education through violence. The sound effect difference is a nice touch.

jokes.text: You don't fight like a girl. Or even a Milhouse. I don't know what is 'Milhouse'.
jokes.punchline: Bart's friend being used as universal weakness standard
jokes.explanation: Good character reference humor. Milhouse's reputation preceding him internationally is funny concept.

jokes.text: Quit going for my groin! No groin, no krav maga. Hello, groin.
jokes.punchline: Girl explaining then demonstrating groin attack philosophy
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with educational element. The casual 'Hello, groin' is a nice threatening touch.

jokes.text: Or as he's known here, 'the man in the underpants'. Beautiful tachtoonim. What abs! He was a real good-looking one. Nice hair. Conditioner? I think so.
jokes.setup: Tour guide describing Jesus at holy site
jokes.punchline: Focusing on Jesus's physical appearance and grooming
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor reducing religious iconography to superficial commentary. The conditioner speculation is particularly funny.

jokes.text: And thank you again for the challenge of Homer Simpson. We've all got our crosses to bear. Me Homer, you... the cross.
jokes.setup: Ned praying about dealing with Homer
jokes.punchline: Comparing his burden to Jesus's literal crucifixion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant religious wordplay. Ned's self-awareness about comparing his problems to crucifixion is perfectly in character and clever.

jokes.text: It's so nice and cool in the tomb of the unknown savior. Unknown?! This is the tomb of the most famous man who ever lived! Porky pig?! Porky pig's not a man! He's a pig, and he's not even a real pig!
jokes.punchline: Series of corrections ending with cartoon character analysis
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation comedy. Homer's complete cultural ignorance paired with Ned's increasingly desperate corrections creates perfect absurdist spiral.

jokes.text: You come all the way to Jerusalem, the happiest place on earth, and all the photos in your camera are of funny soda pops?!
jokes.punchline: Homer's tourist priorities revealed through photos
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Ned's spiritual expectations vs Homer's consumer tourism. 'Funny soda pops' is perfectly dismissive.

jokes.text: Chickpea fizz! Delete. Delete. Delete.
jokes.setup: Ned trying to delete Homer's tourist photos
jokes.punchline: Homer's delight in ridiculous foreign beverage
jokes.explanation: Good callback to soda pop obsession. 'Chickpea fizz' is appropriately absurd beverage name.

jokes.text: Homer Simpson, you are not worth saving.
jokes.setup: Ned's Christian mission to save Homer
jokes.punchline: Complete abandonment of Christian principles
jokes.explanation: Powerful character moment - Ned's faith breaking down. Complete reversal of his core beliefs makes this both shocking and funny.

jokes.text: But I thought we'd be neighbors in heaven. Goofing on all the new dead people.
jokes.punchline: Homer's vision of afterlife as eternal mockery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - reducing heaven to opportunity for bullying. Shows his complete misunderstanding of spirituality.

jokes.text: A sand horse... car of the desert!
jokes.setup: Homer lost in desert needing transportation
jokes.punchline: Poetic but nonsensical description of car
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay humor. Shows Homer's attempt at poetic description in desperate situation.

jokes.text: Nothing like a tall glass of lukewarm water to Jekyll down this Hyde.
jokes.punchline: Mangling Jekyll and Hyde reference
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer malapropism. Shows his attempt at sophistication going wrong in typical fashion.

jokes.text: Not so fast... We can't transform on the sabbath! Let us power down and contemplate the Torah.
jokes.punchline: Transformers robots observing Jewish sabbath
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cultural mashup humor. Taking American action movie and making it religiously observant is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Thirsty, I am so thirsty and hungry and horny but mostly just thirsty I am so thirsty
jokes.setup: Homer dying of thirst in desert
jokes.punchline: Ranking physical needs with typical Homer priorities
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer physical needs prioritization. The insertion of sexual desire in survival situation is perfectly inappropriate.

jokes.text: So salty! Maybe if I add a little salt.
jokes.punchline: Trying to fix salt problem by adding more salt
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic failure. His solution making the problem worse is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You have been chosen, Homer. Chosen for what, o mighty gherkin?
jokes.punchline: Homer addressing vision as pickle
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor. Homer's dehydration hallucination manifesting as food and his respectful address to it.

jokes.text: I am the Messiah! But you still have our passports, right? Oh, yeah. Got to keep track of those. The Messiah... has the passports.
jokes.punchline: Immediate return to mundane travel logistics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of divine and mundane. The anti-climactic passport responsibility perfectly deflates religious grandeur.

jokes.text: But look not upon her with lust, and do not send her friendly e-mails. That's how it begins.
jokes.punchline: Modern infidelity starting with email
jokes.explanation: Excellent modern take on biblical commandments. Shows how ancient wisdom applies to contemporary infidelity patterns.

jokes.text: Did you notice Dad always gets the disease they write about in the in-flight magazine?
jokes.punchline: Lisa noting Homer's pattern of medical suggestibility
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational humor about hypochondria and airline magazines. Perfect Lisa insight into family patterns.

jokes.text: This one says 'Carolina Panthers'! Messiah says...
jokes.punchline: Credit card branded religious proclamation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier Panthers reference. Mixing divine power with corporate branding is appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: You can hold my gun. I used it in Entebbe. I killed three Ugandans.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning violent military history
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about tourist/military culture clash. Jacob's casual mention of killing adds shocking comedy element.

jokes.text: What, Israeli people are pushy? How about you experience genocides and see how laid-back you are? We were purged from Spain! Throw out of there! They allow everybody, there! But for us, Jews, no flamenco, get out!
jokes.punchline: Historical justification escalating to flamenco discrimination
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation comedy. Moving from historical persecution to absurd flamenco exclusion is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: You stay there, surrounded by your great enemy, Canada! Try Syria for two months, then we'll see who's pushy!
jokes.punchline: Comparing American security to Israeli situation using Canada
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political humor. Using Canada as America's 'threat' to contrast with Israeli security concerns is perfect.

jokes.text: From now on, you shall be called... Chrismujews. I don't know.
jokes.punchline: Awkward portmanteau followed by immediate uncertainty
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay followed by typical Homer self-doubt. The religious unification attempt is appropriately clumsy.

jokes.text: But all of us love chicken. He's right! You can simmer it in a tagine. In a soup, you can boil it! Spread the word! Peace and chicken.
jokes.setup: Homer finding common ground between religions
jokes.punchline: Universal chicken appreciation uniting faiths
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist solution to religious conflict. Reducing complex theology to food preferences is perfectly Homer.

jokes.text: Don't listen to this fat idiot! I am your Messiah! Avert your eyes from my wrinkly arms!
jokes.setup: Another person claiming to be Messiah
jokes.punchline: Divine authority undermined by body shame
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy. Divine proclamation immediately undercut by embarrassing physical reality.

jokes.text: You can't hurt what's already dead. I guess you can.
jokes.punchline: Immediate correction after being hurt
jokes.explanation: Good setup/immediate contradiction structure. Homer's quick acceptance of being wrong is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Can I buy you a $12 turkey sandwich? I'd like that. How about a Greg Norman executive putter from the Sky Mall? You'd get a better deal at a mall back home. They have malls on land?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to make amends with overpriced airport food
jokes.punchline: Escalating to more ridiculous airport purchases and geography confusion
jokes.explanation: Good escalation through increasingly absurd airport commerce. Homer's confusion about land-based malls is perfect.

jokes.text: Why don't you try having your people being thrown out of Spain? Spain! No tapas! I love eating tapas! I love the tiny portions! You don't get too full, but you have a variety of flavors! Not for me! Every day hummus and pita! Occasionally, once a week, a little bit of falafel!
jokes.punchline: Escalating to detailed culinary preferences and complaints
jokes.explanation: Good callback and escalation. Moving from historical persecution to detailed food preferences maintains the absurdist trajectory.

tmdb_overview: The Simpsons family vacation in Israel with Ned Flanders, but Homer does not appreciate any of the culture—until an Israeli tour guide named Jakob shows him around and, in a severe state of dehydration, Homer believes he is the Messiah.
